title: euphorbia esula subsp. maglicensis
intro: Euphorbia esula subsp. maglicensis, also known as the spiny leafy spurge, is a subspecies of spurge native to the Balkan Peninsula. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing pain and inflammation, and has been used to treat conditions such as arthritis and gout. Euphorbia esula subsp. maglicensis can be used in herbal preparations, including infusions, decoctions, and salves, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human use.
